---
title: "Practical and Actionable Qualitative Inquiry"
description: "Study note on Practical and Actionable Qualitative Inquiry in Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods."
book_id: "qualitative-research-evaluation-methods"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "269-270"
citation_key: "patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod20-module-20-practical-purposes-concrete-questions-and-actionable-answers-illuminat:c0005"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 269-270</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Practical and Actionable Qualitative Inquiry" from Michael Quinn Patton's *Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods* [@patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

This section links qualitative inquiry to practical purposes: better questions, clearer interpretations, and answers that can guide action.

## Study Summary

This section links qualitative inquiry to practical purposes: better questions, clearer interpretations, and answers that can guide action.

It emphasizes pragmatic and utilization-focused framing, where the point of inquiry is not only understanding but improvement.

Qualitative methods are presented as especially useful for questions that need explanation, depth, and sensitivity to local circumstances.

## Key Concepts

- Actionable answers
- Pragmatic inquiry
- Utilization focus
- Problem-focused evaluation

## Source Location

- Section path: Part 1 > Chapter 4: Practical and Actionable Qualitative Applications > Module 20
- Evidence records: `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod20-module-20-practical-purposes-concrete-questions-and-actionable-answers-illuminat:c0005`

## Practical Implications

- Phrase qualitative questions so that answers can inform a decision.
- Use fieldwork to understand why performance differs across people, sites, or conditions.
- Report patterns with enough context to support use.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Practical and Actionable Qualitative Inquiry helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
